It is about 104 miles (167 km) long.[[1]](#cite_note-NHD-1) The river begins in [Fond du Lac County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Fond_du_Lac_County,_Wisconsin "Fond du Lac County, Wisconsin") and flows south to [downtown Milwaukee](/wiki/Downtown_Milwaukee "Downtown Milwaukee"), where it empties into [Lake Michigan](/wiki/Lake_Michigan "Lake Michigan"). The **Milwaukee River** is a river in southeastern [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin"), United States. It is about 104 miles (167 km) long.[[1]](#cite_note-NHD-1) The river begins in [Fond du Lac County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Fond_du_Lac_County,_Wisconsin "Fond du Lac County, Wisconsin") and flows south to [downtown Milwaukee](/wiki/Downtown_Milwaukee "Downtown Milwaukee"), where it empties into [Lake Michigan](/wiki/Lake_Michigan "Lake Michigan"). Its tributaries are [Cedar Creek](/wiki/Cedar_Creek_(Wisconsin) "Cedar Creek (Wisconsin)"), the [Menomonee River](/wiki/Menomonee_River "Menomonee River"), and the [Kinnickinnic River](/wiki/Kinnickinnic_River_(Milwaukee_River) "Kinnickinnic River (Milwaukee River)").

## Watershed

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milwaukee_River&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Watershed")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Milwaukeerivermap.png/250px-Milwaukeerivermap.png)](/wiki/File:Milwaukeerivermap.png)

Map of the Milwaukee River watershed.

The Milwaukee River [watershed](/wiki/Drainage_basin "Drainage basin") drains 882 square miles (2,280 km2) in southeastern Wisconsin, including parts of [Dodge](/wiki/Dodge_County,_Wisconsin "Dodge County, Wisconsin"), Fond du Lac, [Milwaukee](/wiki/Milwaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Milwaukee County, Wisconsin"), [Ozaukee](/wiki/Ozaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Ozaukee County, Wisconsin"), [Sheboygan](/wiki/Sheboygan_County,_Wisconsin "Sheboygan County, Wisconsin"), [Washington](/wiki/Washington_County,_Wisconsin "Washington County, Wisconsin") and [Waukesha](/wiki/Waukesha_County,_Wisconsin "Waukesha County, Wisconsin") counties.

The Milwaukee River watershed is part of the Lake Michigan subbasin; this subbasin is itself a part of the [St. Lawrence River](/wiki/St._Lawrence_River "St. Lawrence River") Watershed, which is fed by the [Great Lakes](/wiki/Great_Lakes "Great Lakes").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milwaukee_River&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The Milwaukee River area was populated by [Native Americans](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States") in the time before European settlement. [Jacques Marquette](/wiki/Jacques_Marquette "Jacques Marquette") and [Louis Jolliet](/wiki/Louis_Jolliet "Louis Jolliet") navigated from Lake Michigan through the Milwaukee River on their way to the [Fox River](/wiki/Fox_River_(Illinois_River_tributary) "Fox River (Illinois River tributary)") and the [Mississippi](/wiki/Mississippi_River "Mississippi River"). Previously (circa 1834–35) the river had been known as the "Maynawalky," while the present-day [Menomonee River](/wiki/Menomonee_River "Menomonee River") was known as the "Milwalky".[[2]](#cite_note-An_Accompaniment_to_Mitchell's_Reference_and_Distance_Map_of_the_United_States-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In the early 19th century, three towns were formed across the banks of the Milwaukee and Kinnickinnic rivers: Juneautown by [Solomon Juneau](/wiki/Solomon_Juneau "Solomon Juneau"), Walker's Point by [George H. Walker](/wiki/George_H._Walker "George H. Walker") and Kilbourntown by [Byron Kilbourn](/wiki/Byron_Kilbourn "Byron Kilbourn"). The [quarrel](/wiki/Milwaukee_Bridge_War "Milwaukee Bridge War") over the formation of a bridge across the Milwaukee River was a key point in the merging of the three towns into the city of Milwaukee in 1846.

Once a locus of industry, the river is now the center of a housing boom. New condos now crowd the downtown and harbor districts of [Milwaukee](/wiki/Milwaukee "Milwaukee") attracting young professionals to the area. The river is also ribboned with parks as it winds through various neighborhoods. Kayaks and fishing boats share the river with party boats. An extensive Riverwalk featuring art displays, [boat](/wiki/Boat "Boat") launches and restaurants lines its banks in downtown Milwaukee.

## Bridges

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milwaukee_River&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Bridges")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/Milwaukee_November_2022_11_%28Milwaukee_River_from_Milwaukee_Skywalk%29.jpg/330px-Milwaukee_November_2022_11_%28Milwaukee_River_from_Milwaukee_Skywalk%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Milwaukee_November_2022_11_(Milwaukee_River_from_Milwaukee_Skywalk).jpg)

The Milwaukee River as it goes through downtown Milwaukee crossed by the Wisconsin Avenue bridge

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ac/Milwaukee_River_frozen_over.jpg/330px-Milwaukee_River_frozen_over.jpg)](/wiki/File:Milwaukee_River_frozen_over.jpg)

The Milwaukee River frozen over as it is crossed by the Saint Paul Avenue bridge

The Milwaukee River has numerous [movable bridges](/wiki/Movable_bridge "Movable bridge") spanning it, allowing for pedestrian and vehicular traffic. These bridges include several different types, including [bascule](/wiki/Bascule_bridge "Bascule bridge") and hydraulically powered [table bridges](/wiki/Table_bridge "Table bridge"). There are also many fixed bridges, as well as several pedestrian-only and railroad [trestles](/wiki/Trestle_bridge "Trestle bridge").

The following is a partial list of bridges that cross the river, from north to south:

* Brown Deer Road Bridge
* Range Line Road Bridge
* Good Hope Road Bridge
* Green Tree Road Bridge
* Bender Road Bridge
* Silver Spring Drive Bridge
* Hampton Avenue Bridge
* I-43 Bridge
* Port Washington Road Bridge
* Capitol Drive Bridge
* Locust Street Bridge
* North Avenue Bridge (Milwaukee)|North Avenue Bridge
* North-Humboldt Pedestrian Bridge
* Humboldt Street Bridge
* Holton Street Viaduct (1926)
* Pleasant Street Bridge
* Cherry Street Bridge
* McKinley Avenue Bridge aka Knapp Street Bridge
* Juneau Avenue Bridge
* Highland Avenue Pedestrian Bridge
* State Street Bridge (Milwaukee)|State Street Bridge
* Kilbourn Avenue Bridge
* Wells Street Bridge (Milwaukee)|Wells Street Bridge
* Wisconsin Avenue Bridge
* Michigan Street Bridge
* Clybourn Street Bridge
* I-794 Bridge
* Saint Paul Avenue Bridge
* Water Street Bridge
* Broadway Bridge aka Milwaukee Street Bridge
* [Hoan Bridge](/wiki/Hoan_Bridge "Hoan Bridge")

There are also several [Union Pacific](/wiki/Union_Pacific_Railroad "Union Pacific Railroad") (former [Chicago and North Western Railway](/wiki/Chicago_and_North_Western_Railway "Chicago and North Western Railway")) railroad bridges crossing the Milwaukee River, including:

* north of Bender Road
* south of Silver Spring Drive
* Railroad Swing Bridge #1556 (1915)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
